title: Studying chirality imbalance with quantum algorithms
author_string: Alexander M. Czajka, Zhong-Bo Kang, Yuxuan Tee, Fanyi Zhao
abstract: To describe the chiral magnetic effect, the chiral chemical potential $μ_5$ is introduced to imitate the impact of topological charge changing transitions in the quark-gluon plasma under the influence of an external magnetic field. We employ the (1+1) dimensional Nambu-Jona-Lasinio (NJL) model to study the chiral phase structure and chirality charge density of strongly interacting matter with finite chiral chemical potential $μ_5$ in a quantum simulator. By performing the Quantum imaginary time evolution (QITE) algorithm, we simulate the (1+1) dimensional NJL model on the lattice at various temperature $T$ and chemical potentials $μ$, $μ_5$ and find that the quantum simulations are in good agreement with analytical calculations as well as exact diagonalization of the lattice Hamiltonian.
